messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "prelet-lockin-serviceable-window",
 "title": "2026 demand is already specified: the serviceable haircut ignores that reachability has a clock",
 "narrative": "serviceableFactor 0.55 is framed as a channel-and-regulatory reach haircut, as if reach were a static property. But for DC electrical gear, the demand that will invoice in the next 12 months was specified at design stage — the model's own cross-check admits 12-24 month procurement lock, and the corpus shows lock-in occurring far earlier (transformer lead times of 48-60 months, hyperscale cycles up to 4-7 years). Meanwhile the entrant's EU compliance stack (WEEE, CE, TÜV, RoHS, plus EN 50600-compatible documentation) consumes 6-9 months from day 0. The intersection of 'demand contestable in months 1-12' and 'entrant qualified to bid' is a fraction of the static 0.55 — the serviceable share within the YAM window is a different, smaller number than the serviceable share of the market in steady state.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "serviceableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Rack-power specifications for capacity landing in 2026 were locked at design/procurement stage well before the venture's launch date — the model's own cross-check concedes a 12-24 month lock window, and the growth the market shape describes is precisely this pre-committed pipeline",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor",
    "ledger:shape.cagr"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The entrant cannot bid even the contestable residue until its compliance stack clears: the full EU entry set (WEEE, CE, TÜV, Grüner Punkt, RoHS, EORI) is broader than the ledger's named certs and runs 6-9 months, and EN 50600 facility-standard compatibility must be documented for operator RFQs",
   "refs": [
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c13",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c11",
    "profile:Stage"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "serviceableFactor 0.55 counts demand that is temporally unreachable within the first 12 months; the effective serviceable share for the YAM window — demand both uncommitted and open to a newly-certified vendor — is closer to 0.35, and the node has no bottom-up cross-check to catch this",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:serviceableFactor",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c1"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The serviceable haircut looks handled because it already discounts 45% of the TAM and cites the regulatory stack. What the static number hides is a timing dimension: 0.55 may be right for the market in general and simultaneously wrong for every month of the venture's first year, because 'reachable' and 'reachable now' are different sets. A team reviewing the number checks its level, not its clock.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "Three or more CE operator RFQs for rack PDUs in the next two quarters that (a) accept vendors whose certifications complete during the tender process and (b) award within 9 months of publication.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.45,
  "rationale": "Base rate for enterprise/DC hardware: sales cycles of 12-18 months and certification of 6-9 months are well-established for the reference class; the open question is only what fraction of CE demand is genuinely spot-contestable, which no source quantifies — the absence itself is the risk.",
  "basis": "base-rate"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.35,
   "note": "Static reach haircut tightened to window-adjusted reach: demand that is both uncommitted and open to a vendor whose certification completes mid-year"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Time from RFQ publication to award for rack-power line items in CE",
   "where": "TED (Tenders Electronic Daily) and major CE colo operator procurement portals (e.g., NTT, Vantage, Data4 supplier registration pages)",
   "threshold": "Median award cycle >9 months, or supplier prequalification requiring completed certification at bid time",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Quoted lead time for the venture's own TÜV/CE certification slots",
   "where": "TÜV NORD / TÜV SÜD test-lab booking confirmations and notified-body queue quotes",
   "threshold": "Certification completion date beyond month 8 of the launch year",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission a bottom-up channel-coverage model (named CE accounts × spec-lock status × certification-at-bid requirements) to produce a window-adjusted serviceable estimate",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "note": "The ledger's promotionPath names this model; adding the spec-lock dimension is the marginal work that prices the clock"
  },
  {
   "action": "Start the full EU certification stack before launch and pre-register as a supplier on operator procurement portals so month-1 of the commercial window is not month-1 of the compliance clock",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Converts sequential cert→bid timeline into parallel; recovers 4-6 months of the window"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "base-rate-analogy",
 "riskId": "risk.base-rate-analogy.prelet-lockin-serviceable-window"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

But for DC electrical gear, the demand that will invoice in the next 12 months was specified at design stage — the model's own cross-check admits 12-24 month procurement lock, and the corpus shows lock-in occurring far earlier (transformer lead times of 48-60 months, hyperscale cycles up to 4-7 years). adding the spec-lock dimension is the marginal work that prices the clock"},{"action":"Start the full EU certification stack before launch and pre-register as a supplier on operator procurement portals so month-1 of the commercial window is not month-1 of the compliance clock","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Converts sequential cert→bid timeline into parallel; recovers 4-6 months of the window"}]}
